title: SSRF, DNS Rebinding, Injection, Response Validation, and Least Authority · Go Client Systems · Runtime Recall
summary: A security reviewer reconstructing an expired incident handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for ssrf, dns rebinding, injection, response validation, and least authority, producing defense-in-depth outbound request policy. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a Go 1.26 net/http defense-in-depth outbound request policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction,…
objectives: Allow-list required schemes, ports, hosts, and destinations and enforce network egress limits for server-side fetching. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Address DNS rebinding and validation-to-connect races by binding policy to resolved and connected destinations. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Reject control characters and unsafe dynamic structure in targets and fields and rely on maintained encoders. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Ssrf Destination Policy · changed case · Review Ssrf Destination Policy
steps.instruction:
review case — Go security handoff client receives https://127.0.0.1:8002/meta, a decimal IP alias, a hostname that rebinds, and a redirect to file://. Enforce scheme, resolution, connected-address, redirect, response, egress, and privacy defenses. Competency probe: Allow-list required schemes, ports, hosts, and destinations and enforce network egress limits for server-side fetching. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
